author: Dana Edwards

decisions.title: Creation selection is enabled or suspended
decisions.decision: Each resource kind has one authenticated CreationSelection: Enabled(CurrentPolicyId) or Suspended. Creation is permitted only under the exact selected current policy; Suspended permits no creation.
rejected_alternatives: require an active creation policy during emergency containment
allow any accepted predecessor to create
caller-selected creation policy

decisions.title: Predecessors grant narrow compatibility
decisions.decision: AcceptedPredecessor policies may validate existing resources only for explicitly permitted reference or consumption. Disposition alone grants no creation, mint, burn, reward, transformation, or verifier-fallback authority.
rejected_alternatives: predecessor creation during a grace window
implicit authority from semantic-content similarity

decisions.title: Hard revocation prioritizes containment
decisions.decision: HardRevoked policies authorize no normal use from the effective authenticated snapshot onward. Revoking the selected creation policy must atomically select an authorized replacement or set creation to Suspended. Recovery requires a separate governed rule.
rejected_alternatives: revocation affects creation only
automatic migration on revocation
leave a revoked policy selected

decisions.title: Uncommitted authority is exact-context-bound
decisions.decision: Any change to pre-state root, state version, policy root, logical epoch, ordering context, crypto suite, or accumulator profile invalidates all uncommitted prevalidation results, facts, drafts, admissions, and plans from the old context.
rejected_alternatives: reuse when policy contents appear equivalent
bind only state version
bind wall-clock time instead of authenticated logical context

decisions.title: Verifier facts bind class, snapshot, release, and statement
decisions.decision: A sealed fact is usable only for its exact lifecycle class, parent binding, claim and child statement, policy snapshot, validation context, verifier policy, program or key, verifier release, authenticated output, and validity window. Production authority accepts only Production-mode policies.
rejected_alternatives: fact authority from policy ID equality alone
Development or Test receipts accepted under matching bytes
one fact type for admission and postcommit receipts

decisions.title: Effect commitment is acyclic
decisions.decision: Finalization derives bounded SemanticEffects. Its root is committed by the journal; the journal hash is then committed by replay and commit metadata; the atomic commit bundle contains semantic effects plus metadata. No hash preimage contains itself transitively.
rejected_alternatives: include replay metadata in the effects root that the replay record references
storage adapter adds implicit authority-bearing writes
caller-supplied effect totals are authoritative

decisions.title: Replay classification and commit share one linearization boundary
decisions.decision: Replay lookup, exact-versus-conflicting classification, freshness comparison, policy-selected admission, and a new commit execute within one serializable transaction or refinement-equivalent linearizable critical section.
rejected_alternatives: replay lookup outside the commit transaction
separate non-atomic freshness checks
journal accepted before durability

decisions.title: Exact retry is read-only idempotent success
decisions.decision: When the durable replay record exactly matches transition identity, journal hash, and semantic-effects root, retry returns AlreadyCommitted(existing AcceptedJournal) and performs no write.
rejected_alternatives: reject every retry
reapply effects when the old context was fresh
return success from transition identity alone

decisions.title: Mismatched replay identity is a conflict
decisions.decision: An existing transition identity with a different journal hash, semantic-effects root, or semantic identity returns TransitionReplayConflict and performs no write.
rejected_alternatives: first writer silently authorizes later contents
last writer overwrites the replay record

decisions.title: Admission and postcommit facts are separate authority classes
decisions.decision: Admission binds a JournalDraft before commit; postcommit aggregation binds an AcceptedJournal after durable commit. Neither can substitute for the other.
rejected_alternatives: one generic recursive receipt type
postcommit proof retroactively authorizes a transition
admission proof implies durability

decisions.title: External delivery remains at-least-once
decisions.decision: ZRM may atomically persist a transition-derived outbox entry, but external delivery is at-least-once unless the receiver supplies an idempotency contract.
rejected_alternatives: treat network send as part of database atomicity
drop messages after uncertain acknowledgement

decisions.title: Policy rollback is a new version
decisions.decision: Reactivating earlier policy contents is a new governance update with a new state version, machine-state root, and validation context. An old authenticated snapshot tuple is never recreated.
rejected_alternatives: restore the old root and version
allow outstanding plans to resume after rollback

decisions.title: Indeterminate durability returns OutcomeUnknown
decisions.decision: A transport, I/O, or durability failure that prevents authoritative classification returns OutcomeUnknown with an inert retry descriptor. It is never reported as confirmed rejection or success.
rejected_alternatives: map timeout to rejection
assume failure means no write
retry by reusing an authority-bearing commit plan

decisions.title: Policy update changes the complete authenticated context
decisions.decision: A policy update atomically derives and persists the new policy root, incremented state version, new machine-state root, new validation-context hash, governance replay record, and governance audit record.
rejected_alternatives: change policy root without machine-state root
reuse an earlier context tuple
write governance audit outside the atomic update

non_claims: not approved protocol semantics until independent review and maintainer decision
internal bounded evidence is not publicly replayable from this repository
no canonical byte or hash freeze for new objects
no concrete implementation or refinement proof
no automatic recovery for hard-revoked resources
